perturbation.original_text: The Company shall reimburse Consultant, in accordance with Company policy, for all reasonable expenses incurred by Consultant in performing the Services pursuant to this Agreement, but only if Consultant receives written consent from an authorized agent of the Company prior to incurring such expenses and submits receipts for such expenses to the Company in accordance with the Company's general expense reimbursement policies.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company may, at its sole discretion, reimburse Consultant for expenses. Reimbursement shall be subject to internal approvals.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates expense reimbursement based on 'Company policy' with prior written consent and receipt submission. The modified text replaces this with discretionary reimbursement 'at its sole discretion' and 'subject to internal approvals'. This creates an in-text contradiction because later, section 7.3(a) still refers to reimbursable expenses under Article 1, implying an obligation when the modified Article 1 makes it discretionary, creating uncertainty about which version is enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon any termination, all rights and duties of the Company and Consultant toward each other shall cease except: (a) The Company will pay, within thirty (30) days after the effective date of termination, all amounts owing to Consultant for Services completed and accepted by the Company prior to the termination date and related reimbursable expenses, if any, submitted in accordance with the Company's policies and in accordance with the provisions of Article 1 of this Agreement; and (b) Article 2 (Confidentiality), Article 3 (Ownership), Section 4.2 (Covenant Not to Compete), Section 4.3 (Non-Solicitation), Section 4.4 (Non-Circumvention), Article 5 (Return of Company Materials), Article 7 (Term and Termination), Article 8 (Independent Contractor Relationship), Article 9 (Indemnification), Article 10 (Limitation of Liability), Article 11 (Arbitration and Equitable Relief), and Article 12 (Miscellaneous) will survive termination or expiration of this Agreement in accordance with their terms.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates expense reimbursement based on Company policy, prior consent, and receipt submission. The changed text makes reimbursement discretionary at the Company's sole discretion. This directly contradicts section 7.3(a), which states the Company 'will pay' reimbursable expenses 'in accordance with the provisions of Article 1'. The modified Article 1 no longer provides clear, mandatory reimbursement terms, creating a direct conflict regarding whether reimbursement is obligatory or optional, thus causing a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant agrees that all right, title, and interest in and to any material, notes, records, drawings, designs, inventions, improvements, developments, discoveries and trade secrets conceived, discovered, authored, invented, developed or reduced to practice by Consultant, solely or in collaboration with others, whether or not patentable or copyrightable, during the term of this Agreement and arising out of, or in connection with, performing the Services under this Agreement and any copyrights, patents, trade secrets, mask work rights or other intellectual property rights relating to the foregoing (collectively, "Inventions"), are the sole property of the Company. Consultant also agrees to promptly make full written disclosure to the Company of any Inventions and to deliver and assign (or cause to be assigned) and irrevocably assigns fully to the Company all right, title and interest in and to the Inventions.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant agrees that all right, title, and interest in and to any material, notes, records, drawings, designs, inventions, improvements, developments, discoveries and trade secrets conceived, discovered, authored, invented, developed or reduced to practice by Consultant, solely, whether or not patentable or copyrightable, during the term of this Agreement and arising out of, or in connection with, performing the Services under this Agreement and any copyrights, patents, trade secrets, mask work rights or other intellectual property rights relating to the foregoing (collectively, "Inventions"), are subject to negotiation for ownership between Consultant and the Company. Consultant agrees to promptly make full written disclosure to the Company of any Inventions and to deliver and assign (or cause to be assigned) to the company based on a future agreed-upon negotiation.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that all inventions are the sole property of the Company. The modified text introduces ambiguity by stating that ownership of inventions is 'subject to negotiation'. This is an in-text contradiction because Section 3.5 still requires the Consultant to assist the Company in securing its rights in Inventions, implying the Company already owns them, even though the ownership is now under negotiation according to the modified Section 3.1. The term irrevocably assigns has been removed from the text, creating an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consultant agrees to assist Company, or its designee, at the Company's expense, in every proper way to secure the Company's rights in Inventions in any and all countries, including the disclosure to the Company of all pertinent information and data with respect thereto, the execution of all applications, specifications, oaths, assignments and all other instruments that the Company may deem necessary in order to apply for, register, obtain, maintain, defend, and enforce such rights, and in order to deliver, assign and convey to the Company, its successors, assigns and nominees the sole and exclusive right, title, and interest in and to all Inventions and testifying in a suit or other proceeding relating to such Inventions. Consultant further agrees that Consultant's obligations under this Section 3.5 shall continue after the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction by stating that ownership of inventions is 'subject to negotiation', while the contradicted text (Section 3.5) requires the Consultant to assist the Company in securing 'the Company's rights in Inventions'. This implies the Company already owns the rights, directly conflicting with the notion of negotiable ownership. The removal of 'irrevocably assigns' further strengthens this contradiction, as the consultant is no longer obligated to fully transfer rights.

perturbation.original_text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement, with or without cause, upon giving the other party thirty (30) days prior written notice of such termination pursuant to Section 12.7 of this Agreement. The Company may terminate this Agreement immediately and without prior notice if Consultant refuses to or is unable to perform the Services or is in breach of any material provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement, with or without cause, upon mutual agreement. The Company may terminate this Agreement immediately if Consultant refuses to perform the Services.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows termination with 30 days written notice. The modified version states termination can occur upon 'mutual agreement'. Section 7.3 (a) outlines payments owed to the consultant upon termination of the agreement, referencing the termination conditions defined in the original Section 7.2. However, the modified version's requirement for 'mutual agreement' and omitting the 30-day notice period contradicts this, as there are no clear conditions in case of termination. This contradiction creates uncertainty about when and how the consultant will be paid post-termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon any termination, all rights and duties of the Company and Consultant toward each other shall cease except: (a) The Company will pay, within thirty (30) days after the effective date of termination, all amounts owing to Consultant for Services completed and accepted by the Company prior to the termination date and related reimbursable expenses, if any, submitted in accordance with the Company's policies and in accordance with the provisions of Article 1 of this Agreement; and (b) Article 2 (Confidentiality), Article 3 (Ownership), Section 4.2 (Covenant Not to Compete), Section 4.3 (Non-Solicitation), Section 4.4 (Non-Circumvention), Article 5 (Return of Company Materials), Article 7 (Term and Termination), Article 8 (Independent Contractor Relationship), Article 9 (Indemnification), Article 10 (Limitation of Liability), Article 11 (Arbitration and Equitable Relief), and Article 12 (Miscellaneous) will survive termination or expiration of this Agreement in accordance with their terms.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows termination with 30 days' notice. The changed text replaces this with 'mutual agreement', eliminating the defined termination conditions. The contradicted text, Section 7.3(a), specifies payments owed to the consultant upon termination, referencing the original termination conditions. By removing the 30-day notice and replacing it with 'mutual agreement', the changed text creates a direct contradiction. It becomes unclear how and when the consultant will be paid upon termination, directly conflicting with the explicit payment terms outlined in Section 7.3(a) and rendering the provision uncertain and potentially unenforceable. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as essential language about termination conditions has been removed, creating a conflict with obligations stated elsewhere.
